strengths: 15+ years of fullstack experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems — far exceeds the 6-year minimum, with direct TypeScript/Node.js/React/AWS work at Optilyz and re:cinq
Active AI agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — directly matches the 'AI agent development' requirement
Demonstrated cloud and containerization depth: AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes-deployable platform at GISCollective, Docker setup at Altom — covers the cloud/container competency concretely
concerns: Nest.js — the primary backend framework listed in the stack — appears nowhere in the resume; Node.js is present but the framework-level fit is unverified
Kotlin is listed as a secondary requirement and Java appears only in embedded/robotic-arm context at Altom (2016–2018); no modern JVM backend service work is evidenced

Testing frameworks Vitest and Playwright are not mentioned; TDD practice is cited and a D-language assertion library was built, but fit to the specified JS/TS testing toolchain is assumed, not demonstrated
response.justification: Bogdan presents a strong overall profile for this role: his current position at re:cinq explicitly covers fullstack TypeScript/Node.js work and AI-agent infrastructure, which is a differentiating requirement few candidates will meet. His cloud and containerization experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker) is evidenced across multiple roles rather than just listed as a skill. The primary gaps are Nest.js and the specified testing frameworks (Vitest, Playwright) — neither appears in the resume — and Kotlin, which is absent entirely. These are learnable gaps for a candidate at this seniority level, but they are real unknowns. The breadth of his open-source work and workshop activity suggests strong engineering communication and craft. On balance, the evidence for the high-weight competencies (AI agent work, fullstack seniority, cloud) outweighs the framework-level gaps, and the gaps are worth probing in an interview rather than screening out over.
